title: Muting does not stop an in-flight TTS PA announcement
detail: The `set muted(value)` setter (lines 289-296) updates master/music/machine WebAudio gains but never touches the SpeechSynthesis TTS pipeline: `set muted(value: boolean) { this._muted = value; this.updateMasterVolume(); this.updateMusicVolume(); this.updateMachineVolumes(); this.saveSettings(); this.notifyListeners(); }`. TTS announcements are spoken via `window.speechSynthesis.speak(utterance)` (line 5620), which is NOT routed through the WebAudio gain graph, so zeroing gains does not silence it. By contrast `set ttsEnabled(false)` calls `this.stopTTS()` (line 5329). The queue-processing guard at line 5486 (`if (!this._ttsEnabled || this._muted)`) and the scheduler guards already block NEW announcements when muted, so the bug is bounded to exactly ONE already-speaking utterance continuing to talk after the user mutes. Clearly-wrong UX: muting should silence the voice immediately.
fix: Call `this.stopTTS()` inside the muted setter when value===true (stopTTS already cancels speechSynthesis, clears the queue, and clears the chime timeout). This changes mute semantics, so it needs owner sign-off.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants B: AmbientDetails + shared + aiEngine + audioManager

title: AI engine teardown leaks pending safety-report resolution timeouts
detail: `initializeAIEngine()`'s returned cleanup (lines 3390-3398) clears `loopInterval` but never clears the module-level `activeResolutionTimeouts` map (declared line 3978, populated line 4109 with 5000ms setTimeouts via `activeResolutionTimeouts.set(report.id, timeoutId)`). After teardown these timeouts still fire and call `useSafetyReportStore.getState().resolveSafetyReport(...)` against a store that may belong to a torn-down session. The debounceTimeout in initializeDecisionOutcomeTracking IS cleaned up (line 3028) — this map is the inconsistent gap. Low impact (one-shot 5s timers, store calls are idempotent) but a genuine lifecycle leak.
fix: In the cleanup closure, add: `for (const id of activeResolutionTimeouts.keys()) clearTimeout(activeResolutionTimeouts.get(id)!); activeResolutionTimeouts.clear();` before/after clearing loopInterval. Stays inside the file; no behavior change in normal operation.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants B: AmbientDetails + shared + aiEngine + audioManager

title: Strategic Gemini failures never trigger API exponential backoff
detail: `generateStrategicDecision()` swallows its own errors: `catch (error) { logger.error('[Strategic] Decision generation failed:', error); setStrategicThinking(false); return null; }` (lines 3951-3955) and returns null instead of rethrowing. The AI-loop caller wraps it in try/catch (lines 3292-3308) expecting to call `recordApiFailure()` on throw — but because the error is swallowed and null returned, the loop sees a benign null and `recordApiFailure()` (line 3304) never runs for strategic API errors. So repeated Gemini failures on the strategic path do not increment apiBackoff.consecutiveFailures, defeating the backoff for that layer. NOTE: do NOT route the loop-level null to recordApiFailure — null is also the benign no-op return (not active / not connected / no priorities).
fix: Add `recordApiFailure();` inside the catch block at line 3952 (the genuine-error site), alongside the existing logger.error. Local, single-file.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants B: AmbientDetails + shared + aiEngine + audioManager

title: Roof-leak drip uses setState every animation frame (stale read, re-render churn)
detail: RoofLeakPuddle's animation callback (lines 4172-4193) calls `setDropY((prev) => ...)` every frame, contradicting this file's own stated design (line 915 comment: condensation is 'ref-based animation (no setState in useFrame)'). Per-frame setState forces a React re-render of the component each frame. Additionally `dropRef.current.position.y = dropY` (line 4183) reads the stale closure `dropY`, not the freshly computed `newY` returned from the updater, so the mesh visually lags the state by one animation tick. Throttled to every 3rd frame by the manager, so impact is modest, but it is the only per-frame setState in the centralized-animation file.
fix: Convert dropY to a useRef (e.g. dropYRef.current) updated/read inside the callback, matching the condensation-drip pattern, eliminating per-frame setState and the stale read. Visual/3D change — verify the drip still animates; prefer owner review.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants B: AmbientDetails + shared + aiEngine + audioManager

title: Door-toggle interval restarts on every door state change (timing churn)
detail: AmbientDetailsGroup's door interval useEffect (lines 4532-4556) lists `[doorStates]` as its dependency. Each door toggle calls `setDoorStates(...)`, which changes doorStates, which tears down and recreates the interval — re-rolling the random `15000 + Math.random()*30000` delay on every fire. Net effect: the 'periodic' door behavior never settles to its intended cadence; the timer is effectively reset each cycle. (Sound logic itself is correct: pre-flip `doorStates[doorId]===true` -> playDoorClose, ===false -> playDoorOpen, which matches the open/close transition.) Not a correctness defect in output, only timing/perf.
fix: Use a functional setState inside the callback to read current door state and drop `doorStates` from deps (use [] so the interval is created once), or read state from a ref. Local single-file change; low risk but alters door cadence slightly, so light owner awareness.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants B: AmbientDetails + shared + aiEngine + audioManager

title: Production TTS pipeline emits ~14 unconditional console.log debug lines
detail: The announcement/TTS path logs verbosely to the raw console regardless of environment: e.g. line 5480 `console.log('[TTS] Queue empty, stopping')`, 5487 `console.log('[TTS] Blocked in queue processing:', {...})`, 5510, 5559 `console.log('[TTS] Speech STARTED')`, 5565, 5593, 5602, 5614-5619, 5624. These use `console.log` directly rather than the project's gated `audioLog`/`logger` abstraction used elsewhere in the same file (e.g. audioLog.warn at 5580/5631). On every PA announcement this spams the production console at millos.net. Not a crash, but console noise and minor info leak of internal state.
fix: Route these through the existing gated logger (audioLog.debug) or guard with import.meta.env.DEV. Mechanical, no runtime UX change.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants B: AmbientDetails + shared + aiEngine + audioManager
